title: Compose Development, Overrides, Profiles, Watch, and Test Workflows · Container Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective development and test Compose workflow matrix; isolate the first causal fault in compose development, overrides, profiles, watch, and test workflows and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in development and test Compose workflow matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.
Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies.
Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Compose Files Merge Reset
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug override bind-mounts production paths, watch rebuilds on generated files, and dev and test profiles silently change dependencies. Render every file combination and isolate disposable state. Competency probe: Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Compose Config Dry Run
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-compose-config-dry-run-p494z9". Tailor its compose.dev.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Compose Config Dry Run.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Compose Config Dry Run into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Compose Config Dry Run, keep Docker Compose Config Dry Run connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-compose-config-dry-run-p494z9
  - competency: docker-compose-config-dry-run
    artifact: compose.dev.yaml
    decision: compose config dry run
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: native amd64 engine with a cold content store
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: p494z9
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      develop:
      watch:
      - action: sync
      path: ./src
      profiles: [dev]
      test:
      profiles: [test]
    verify: docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.dev.yaml config --profiles
    failure: Reject production bind mounts, hidden override precedence, watch-triggered dependency churn, or a profile that changes the core dependency contract.
    repair: Render every file combination, scope development-only state, select sync versus rebuild deliberately, and prove test teardown is isolated.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
